message:
fix(fcp-kubernetes): regression-guard comments on non-const str-match helpers (br-q0y8h)

## Why

Bead flywheel_connectors-q0y8h tracked a repeat-offender: the four
connector helpers `is_secret_mutation`, `is_system_namespace`,
`is_shell_binary`, and `approval_for_operation` can tempt a
"make everything const" refactor because they are pure and return
`bool` / `Option<ApprovalMode>`. But their bodies are `matches!` over
`&str` literals, and `str` equality inside `const fn` still requires
unstable const-trait support on the current nightly. The last time
someone re-added `const` the whole `fcp-e2e` dev-dependency graph
stopped compiling (E0658 "cannot match on `str` in constant
functions") and the prior fix in f8712801 dropped the `const` qualifier.

Audit of current `connectors/kubernetes/src/connector.rs` confirms
the four helpers are already non-const: `is_secret_mutation` at
:1421, `is_system_namespace` at :1428, `is_shell_binary` at :1449,
`approval_for_operation` at :2957. `fcp-kubernetes` builds cleanly
and the full lib suite is 514/514. Nothing to code-fix — but the
bead stays open until the regression is visibly guarded, because
rustfmt sweeps and mechanical "add const where possible" lint
auto-fixes will try again.

## What

One block comment above `is_secret_mutation` (covering all four
`str`-match helpers via proximity) and one one-line comment above
`approval_for_operation` (which is ~1500 lines away). Both explicitly
call out:
  - the bead id + prior fix commit (f8712801) so the next reader
    lands in the history
  - the exact compiler error (E0658) they'll see if they revert
  - the reason this can't move to `const` until const-trait `PartialEq`
    stabilises

A static_assert-style compile check isn't available for "not-const";
the comment is the minimum-invasive regression guard.

## Verified

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p2-r1 cargo build -p fcp-kubernetes
  → clean

  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-p2-r1 cargo test -p fcp-kubernetes --lib
  → 514/514 pass

`fcp-e2e --tests` has its own unrelated `CapabilityToken` /
`HandshakeRequest` signature-mismatch breakage that is NOT caused by
this bead and is out of scope here (tracked separately).

Closes flywheel_connectors-q0y8h.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
